prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a completely black frame, with no visible objects or text.

Around the middle of the clip, the scene changes to a black-and-white interior that looks like an office or study. Shelving with books and objects is visible on the left, and a desk or table with papers and a telephone-like object appears on the right. A dark-clothed person or figure is partially visible in the center foreground, though their face and full body are not clearly shown. Large on-screen text appears on the right reading: “Peptans settles acid stomach.”

The next scene shifts to a product-focused display. A bottle labeled “Liquid Peptans” is shown prominently near the center, with a product box or carton visible to the right. On the left, large text reads: “Breaks the ‘Trouble Triangle’.” The bottle label includes additional claims in smaller print, but not all of it is fully legible in the frame.

The final frame is a close-up of the product packaging. The box reads “LIQUID Peptans” in large type. Below that, inside a dark oval, the package lists benefits: “SETTLES ACID STOMACH,” “CALMS DIGESTIVE NERVES,” and “RELIEVES ACHY HEAD.” Near the bottom, smaller text identifies “THE NORWICH PHARMACAL COMPANY.”
